# vellora

> HTML to PDF for Node.js, native and browserless by default.

vellora renders generated HTML documents such as invoices, receipts, statements, boletos, reports,
and notifications to deterministic PDFs from Node.js. The default renderer runs in-process through a
napi-rs native addon and does not install Puppeteer, Playwright, Chromium, wkhtmltopdf, Python, Java,
or a sidecar service.

Primary links:

- Docs: https://diomalta.github.io/vellora/
- npm package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/vellora
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/diomalta/vellora
- Getting started: https://diomalta.github.io/vellora/guide/getting-started
- Compatibility: https://diomalta.github.io/vellora/compatibility
- Migration guide: https://diomalta.github.io/vellora/migrating

Use vellora when you control the generated document template and want HTML-to-PDF output without
shipping a headless browser in the default runtime. Use a browser renderer when a template requires
JavaScript execution, arbitrary website rendering, or exact Chromium print output.
